body: Make the jobs page more streamlined for mobile phones or create a new scan out page that shows a list of what to pick for a job and see how many are left while also have easy access to the scanner. Also change the device page to have cards for a better overview and less horisontal scrolling on the phone.
title: Better mobile experience

body: Make the inline job requirement updates better by making it possible to search and use a category tree to select the product to update/add to the job.
title: Inline change requirement

body: Saml or oidc to work with entra id, keycloak and other similar services with guides to set up for atleast entra id. Roles should be possible to map from groups in the idp.

body: Rental equipment should also be possible to scan both when I get them from the vendor and when I take them out to a job or being able to do it directly to a job and the same with checking in them to have control over where the rented equipment is.

body: When scanning out a job I should be able to scan out a case and then it should add all the devices inside that case. When checking in it should also be able to check it in but with a list of the content it should include and with an option to have to scan every device in.
title: Scan in and out cases

body: Now there is a big number of tabs in the admin view so we should refactor that to something that scales better when adding even more integrations and other things.
title: Refactor admin view

body: The ability to use something like orbit.js to be able to work offline and having it sync the changes when it comes online. But it when online update should still be instant possibly with websockets.
title: Offline abilities

body: Update and cleanup documentation and readme files to match the current functionality
title: Update documentation

body: Being able to use any s3 or file storage would make deploy easier when we dont have nextcloud available.
title: Remove nextcloud dependency

body:
**Summary**
Provide a stable, documented read API and OpenAPI contract so RentalCore can stop relying on cross-database foreign keys and fetch cable/device metadata from WarehouseCore.

**Background**
RentalCore and WarehouseCore are currently coupled via DB FKs (e.g., `devices.cable_id`). We will decouple: RentalCore keeps job mappings; WarehouseCore is the authoritative product/cable catalog.

**Goals**
- Implement read endpoints required by RentalCore:
  - `GET /admin/cables` (searchable)
  - `GET /admin/cables/{id}`
  - `GET /devices/{id}` (include `cable_id`)
- Publish and validate `docs/openapi.decouple.yml`.
- Enforce API-key auth for service-to-service calls (`X-API-Key` header).
- Provide example responses, error handling (401/404/429/5xx) and basic rate-limit guidance.

**Acceptance criteria**
- `docs/openapi.decouple.yml` added and validated (openapi-cli or swagger).
- Handlers implemented and return JSON matching the schema for sample IDs.
- API-key auth enforced for these endpoints.
- Integration test or smoke test demonstrating RentalCore can fetch cable metadata for an existing `cable_id`.
- README updated with usage and example curl.

**Tasks (suggested)**
- [ ] Add handler `GET /admin/cables` (supports `search`, `connector1`, `connector2`, `type`, `length_min`, `length_max`).
- [ ] Add handler `GET /admin/cables/{id}`.
- [ ] Add handler `GET /devices/{id}` (include `cable_id`).
- [ ] Add API-key middleware and create a service key for RentalCore.
- [ ] Commit `docs/openapi.decouple.yml`.
- [ ] Add unit + integration tests for new handlers.
- [ ] Document auth, example curl, and rollout notes in README.
- [ ] Add simple performance smoke test (suggest 100 rps).

**Example curl**
```
curl -H "X-API-Key: $WAREHOUSE_API_KEY"
"[https://warehousecore.example/api/v1/admin/cables/123](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)"
```


**Risks & Rollback**
- Read-only endpoints — low risk. If performance issues occur, add caching or rate-limits or revoke the service key temporarily.
title: Add service API endpoints + OpenAPI spec to support decoupling

body: Cables are really just product with some added attributes so lets remove the specifics for cables and make it instead possible to add the connector and length fields to all products instead and also make it possible to add other custom fields of different types to products.
title: Merge the cable functionality with products
Convert to case only creates one case even if the mother product had multiple devices

title: Convert product to cables or case

body:
Environment: branch/commit, local dev server at [http://localhost:8082](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
Steps to reproduce:
Run:
Observe HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized with body [{"error":"Unauthorized - No session"}](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
Actual: admin endpoints reject [Authorization: Bearer <token>](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) (401).
Expected: either (A) admin endpoints accept API keys (X-API-Key) / Bearer tokens that map to an active api_keys row, or (B) documentation clarifies admin endpoints require session cookie login.
Suggested change:
Option 1: Extend AuthMiddleware/RequireAdmin to accept API keys (hash with [repository.HashAPIKey](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)) — check api_keys table (reuse logic from warehousecore/internal/middleware/api_key_middleware.go) and allow keys with an admin flag, or
Option 2: Add a new middleware that accepts X-API-Key / [Authorization: Bearer <key>](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) for programmatic admin access (secure, auditable), or
Option 3: Update docs and uploader to always use cookie-based login for admin write operations.
Relevant files to inspect/test:
warehousecore/internal/middleware/api_key_middleware.go
warehousecore/internal/middleware/auth_middleware.go (used for admin routes)
warehousecore/internal/handlers/api_key_handlers.go (API-key creation)
warehousecore/internal/repository/database.go / repository.HashAPIKey (hashing)
Attachments / logs: include the curl verbose output you pasted above and the uploader debug log.
title: Support API-key or Bearer-token auth for admin endpoints (or document current cookie-only requirement)
Bulk edit and delete products and devices
